internal_anchors.details: node test.js passed 3519/3519 — Section 3 covers all href="#…" targets and showTab() arguments. 12 ds-tab-content divs with unique IDs; every showTab() argument resolves to one of them; no orphan tabs.

tab_structure.details: Tab order overview→evaluate→model→selftest→wins→pages→killshots→timestamp→predictions→falsify→ai→refs is continuous. 12 ds-section-nav blocks; first tab prev=null, last tab next=null. Each tab has at least one h1 and one h2; heading hierarchy never skips a level.

external_links.details: DOI/publisher domains skipped per spec (curmudgeon checks them per-WIN). Of 49 data-source URLs hit twice, 46 returned 200, 2 NOAA paths returned 403 to all bot UAs (Akamai/CDN policy — real users get the page; informational only), 1 podaac-tools.jpl.nasa.gov path TCP-timed-out twice from this network (other podaac.jpl.nasa.gov URLs returned 200, so it appears specific to that host).

mismatches.location: data/sections.json part1b TLDR; rendered at docs/index.html ~line 1027 ("The 30 new WINs break into recognizable patterns"). The detail body two paragraphs later correctly templates {{NEW_IN_V51}} → 32, so it reads internally inconsistent.

data_prose_consistency.details: Total wins.json = 71 entries (69 base + 2 sub-IDs WIN-057b/058b). Verdict-bar counts match exactly: Misleading 22, Std Model 18, Self-Contradicted 14, Refuted 9, Not Demonstrated 4, Unfalsifiable 4 (sum = 71, all percentages computed against 71). Scorecard cards 69/0/4/4 use the dome's own claim count and dome_claimed_failures from uncounted-failures.json. Acknowledged-failures placeholder logic verified: silent=4, ack-bucket=6, dome_claimed=4 — scorecard renders dome_claimed (4), correct.

win_detail_consistency.details: All 71 expected winNNN[suffix] anchors found in HTML; 0 missing, 0 extra. WIN-057b and WIN-058b correctly track the dome's number-collision (dome assigns same WIN-NNN to two different claims; our suffix scheme captures both). 8 detail_groups all internally consistent. All wins have tldr_evidence + tldr_verdict; all 12 genuinely-prospective predictions have a tldr.

win_alignment_with_dome_site.details: Fetched john09289.github.io/predictions/wins.html. WIN-001..WIN-069 enumerated. WIN-057 and WIN-058 each carry two distinct claim definitions on the dome side (Two-Zone Disc Topology vs SAA Simultaneous Acceleration; θ=−lon vs Bedrock Induction Ratio). Our 057/057b/058/058b mappings align with both. PROS-001..PROS-008 cross-references noted but not in scope for this check.

progressive_disclosure.details: 351 <details> / 347 <summary> blocks. ps-summary 119 / ks-summary 228 = 347. ps-tldr 119 / ks-tldr 228 = 347. 0 empty TLDRs. 21 h2 elements outside <summary>: all are scorecard headers, top-level subsection headings (3.2 Refuted, 3.3 Self-Contradicted, etc.), or navigational headers — legitimate exceptions per prompt.

theme_color_drift.details: 0 hardcoded light backgrounds (#fff, #f8f9fa, etc.) in sections.json inline styles. 3 occurrences of color:#555 (italic subheaders in part4.html, part5.html, part6.html) — minor; could be replaced with var(--text) but doesn't break dark theme.

discoverability_infrastructure.details: docs/llms.txt (5.6 KB) opens with our review URL framing, mentions the dome model by name, current 71/69 counts. docs/sitemap.xml is valid XML with main URL + llms.txt URL. docs/robots.txt has Allow: / and Sitemap: directive. Meta tags in <head>: description, og:title, og:description, ld+json ClaimReview block all present. data/uncounted-failures.json: 10 entries, all with id/dome_ref/dome_label/what_actually_happened, no duplicate IDs, all FAIL-NNN format.

expansion_tracker_continuity.details: max_id=285, next_id=286 (next_id > max_id, no imminent collision). 284 items, 1 ID gap at EXP-052 (long-standing — single skipped allocation). 202 expansion files on disk, 0 orphans, 0 phantom entries, 0 stale-pending. all coupled-category items either integrated or correctly represented in priority-queue (0 needed, 0 missing).

workspace_only_files.details: 1 real curmudgeon review present on FUSE workspace but not yet in git: WIN-016.c3.json (21 KB, mtime 2026-05-04 05:37 UTC — written ~2 minutes before this integrity run by the curmudgeon). Will be picked up on the next workspace-sync run; no action needed unless the next run >24 h away. Two debris files also present on workspace: WIN-067.c3.json.build.js (empty stray, looks like a name-mangled side-effect from a node script) and EXP-202.json.bak (canonical EXP-202.json present in git, identical size). Recommend cleanup but not data-loss-critical.

workspace_sync_skip_log.details: 0 direction-violation skips in last 24 h. 8 mtime-guard skips (informational only) on category-proposals/CAT-005.json, expansion-tracker.json, applied-patches/*.json, morning-briefing.txt, daily-report — all expected (decider commits these directly to git, FUSE copy is stale).

digest_coverage.details: Regenerated pending-digest.json before reading. 452 review files → 248 unique base IDs, 447 already processed, 0 unprocessed. 0 parse errors. Side note from digest: 235 'under-covered processed' reviews flagging 1265 missing-issues holes — informational only, not in this prompt's scope, surfaced for the decider/tinker.

project_documentation.details: All 22 spot-checked file references in CLAUDE.md exist on disk. Schedule table well-formed (8 agents enumerated). The single bare 69 count ("the model claims 69 confirmed predictions") is the dome's own claim, accompanied by the live query for our review's count, so it complies with the no-hardcoded-counts rule.

build_reproducibility.details: node build.js html in fresh clone vs committed docs/index.html: 1-line diff, the BUILD_DATE stamp ("May 3, 2026" → "May 4, 2026") which is computed at build time from new Date(). Not drift — last published build was on May 3.

issues_found.description: Stale hardcoded count in TLDR: "The 30 new WINs break into recognizable patterns" — actual new_in_v51 = 32. The detail body two paragraphs later correctly templates {{NEW_IN_V51}} = 32, so the same details block is internally inconsistent.
issues_found.location: data/sections.json — part1b → new-content-breakdown TLDR (rendered at docs/index.html line ~1027)
issues_found.suggested_fix: Replace "The 30 new WINs" with "The {{NEW_IN_V51}} new WINs" (or refactor counts in the breakdown — 9+5+5+9 = 28 — to match 32; the breakdown numbers are also stale and should be recomputed). The build-script context already exposes counts.newInV51.

issues_found.description: Curmudgeon review file present on FUSE workspace but not in git. Will be lost if workspace recycles before next workspace-sync run.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Workspace-sync's next run (every 4 h) should commit it. If the agent has been failing, run workspace-sync manually or copy the file into a clone and push.

issues_found.description: Stray debris files on FUSE workspace.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Delete from FUSE (rm both files in the workspace mount). The .build.js suffix on the curmudgeon review looks like a name collision in a node script — worth grepping the curmudgeon prompt for any path-construction that could append '.build.js'.

issues_found.description: podaac-tools.jpl.nasa.gov TCP-timed out twice on port 443. Other podaac.jpl.nasa.gov URLs in the review return 200, so it's specific to that host.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Re-check on next run. NASA Drive has migrated several times since 2024; if persistent, the GRACE ProdSpec doc moved to https://podaac.jpl.nasa.gov/gravity/grace-documentation.

issues_found.description: 3 hardcoded color:#555 declarations in inline styles (part4.html, part5.html, part6.html italic subheaders).
issues_found.location: data/sections.json — part4.html line 103, part5.html line 80, part6.html line 11457
issues_found.suggested_fix: Replace with color:var(--text) or define a utility class. Doesn't break dark theme but defeats theming.

issues_found.description: ID gap at EXP-052 (single skipped allocation). next_id is correct so no collision risk.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Likely intentional or a long-ago aborted item. No action needed; flagged for awareness.

summary: Build is reproducible (1-line diff = build-date stamp). test.js 3519/3519 pass. 71 winNNN[suffix] anchors all present and aligned with dome site (including the WIN-057/058 number-collision tracking). Tab structure, sectionNav chain, heading hierarchy, progressive-disclosure structure, discoverability infrastructure, and expansion tracker all clean. One real moderate finding (stale '30 new WINs' TLDR vs 32 in the detail body — same <details> block contradicts itself), one major workspace-only curmudgeon review awaiting workspace-sync (transient — should clear within 4 h), and a handful of minor cleanup items (stray .build.js / .bak files, three hardcoded #555 greys, one NASA URL TCP-timing-out, single ID gap at EXP-052).
